sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: |5 Insightful Analyst Questions From Ameris Bancorp’s Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: |Ameris Bancorp reported Q4 results that met revenue expectations, driven by higher net interest income and efficient expense management. The company achieved record earnings for 2025, with diluted EPS reaching $6 per share for the first time. Analysts focused on future CRE payoffs, net interest margin expectations, capital deployment, mortgage segment performance, and strategies for deposit growth.

feed.title: Costco launches mobile app ordering for custom cakes and deli trays, easing customer woes
feed.summary: Costco is overhauling its custom cake and deli tray ordering system by introducing mobile app and website options, retiring a decades-old paper form process that customers found inconvenient. This digital move, which executives described as streamlining a "clunky" system, will allow shoppers to customize orders online. The feature is expected to be fully implemented across all locations by the end of 2026, making it easier for members, especially those living far from stores, to place orders.

feed.title: Large businesses driving credit demand growth - Equifax
feed.summary: New data from Equifax indicates that large enterprises are the primary drivers of current credit demand growth, while small and medium-sized businesses (SMEs) are experiencing a more gradual recovery from inflationary pressures. Business credit scores have reached a three-year high due to increased borrowing by large companies, though SME borrowing still lags pre-inflation levels from early 2022. Several key sectors, including hospitality, construction, and retail, show a significant disparity in credit activity between large businesses and SMEs.

feed.title: IBM Expands Entry-Level Hiring Even as AI Reshapes Work
feed.summary: IBM plans to triple its entry-level hiring in 2026, redefining junior roles to work alongside AI rather than being replaced by it. The company is rehauling job descriptions, particularly in software development and human resources, to focus on strategic tasks that leverage human insight while AI handles routine operations. This strategy counters concerns about job displacement and aims to build a future talent pipeline.

feed.title: United States Food Waste Management Market 2026 | Market with
feed.summary: The Food Waste Management Market reached US$69.8 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow to US$136.2 billion by 2032, driven by increasing global food waste, stringent government regulations, environmental awareness, and technological advancements. The report highlights recent industry developments in the United States and Japan, key players, and segmented market analysis by waste type, source, service, and process. North America currently holds the largest share, followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific, with growth fueled by circular economy solutions and sustainability pushes.

feed.title: Applied Materials reportedly fined $252m for unlicensed shipments to China’s SMIC
feed.summary: Applied Materials is reportedly facing a $252 million fine from the U.S. Department of Commerce for illegally shipping semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China's SMIC. The company allegedly routed ion implanters through a South Korean subsidiary to SMIC without the required export licenses, even after SMIC was placed on the U.S. Entity List. This penalty comes amidst ongoing U.S. efforts to restrict China's access to advanced semiconductor technologies due to national security concerns.
